inputs.prompt: Topic: Why most people fail at learning a new language as adults. Format: YouTube long-form. Tone: direct, science-backed. Audience: 28-45, frustrated learners who have tried apps and courses.

Topic: How wastewater epidemiology is being used to track drug consumption patterns in cities by testing sewage samples. Format: YouTube long-form. Tone: serious journalism. Audience: public health professionals and policy wonks.

Topic: Why the Dutch housing crisis is worse than most Europeans realize, despite the Netherlands being one of the wealthiest countries in the EU. Format: Documentary opening. Tone: serious journalism. Audience: European viewers interested in housing policy.
Topic: Something about how technology is changing things. Format: Podcast. Audience: general.

Topic: How Kenya's M-Pesa mobile money system created a parallel financial infrastructure that the formal banking sector is now trying to absorb. Format: YouTube long-form. Tone: business journalism. Audience: fintech professionals and emerging market investors.

Topic: The psychological toll on first responders who repeatedly encounter child abuse cases. Format: Documentary opening. Tone: sombre, respectful, no sensationalism. Audience: social workers, police, healthcare professionals.
Topic: Why nuclear energy is both the safest and most dangerous power source. Format: YouTube Shorts (under 60 seconds). Tone: be funny AND scientifically rigorous. Audience: Gen Z climate activists.

Topic: How South Korea's hagwon (private tutoring academy) industry shapes childhood, family finances, and national anxiety about education. Format: YouTube long-form. Tone: empathetic cultural reportage. Audience: international educators and parents.

Topic: The complete history and current state of desalination technology, from ancient Greek sailors boiling seawater through 1960s reverse osmosis breakthroughs to modern Israeli membrane factories, including energy costs per cubic metre, brine disposal environmental impact, the Saudi NEOM megaproject's planned solar-powered desalination, and why most African coastal cities still lack desalination capacity despite water scarcity. Format: YouTube long-form (target 25 minutes). Tone: measured, optimistic but honest. Audience: engineering students and water policy professionals aged 22-40.
